name: Ken Haedrich's Three Grain Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the butter into 1/4 inch pieces.
2. Lightly butter a large baking sheet, preferably dark metal.
3. In a mixing bowl, combine flour, cornmeal, oats, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
4. Add the unsalted butter and rub or cut it in until the mixture looks like coarse meal.
5. Blend the sour cream and milk in a separate bowl.
6. Make a well in the dry ingredients and add the liquid to the well all at once. Stir the dough until evenly mixed.
7. Sprinkle the dough, your hands, and your work surface with flour. Turn the dough out and gently knead 20 or 30 seconds. Use a little more flour if necessary to keep the dough from sticking.
8. Pat the dough out to a thickness of about 3/4 inch. Cut the dough using 2 or 2 1/4-inch round cutters. Keep the cuts close together.
9. Place the biscuits on the buttered baking sheet and bake at 350 degrees for 13 to 14 minutes until browned and crusty. Serve immediately.
